evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Has some opinions (skip culture day one) but many FAQ answers are bland and hedged.

suggestedPatch.before: Oahu is set up for both, beaches, hikes, and museums are family-proof, but most friend groups will want to set expectations for late dinners and city nightlife if traveling with kids.

evidence: intro_hook 6/10 — The post buries its best sentence ('There's a specific moment on every group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay...') several paragraphs down after a generic two-sentence open that restates the title. The TL;DR and Quick Answer blocks also clutter the opening before the reader gets any narrative pull. The Hanauma Bay moment is vivid and earned; it should be sentence one.

suggestedPatch.target: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
suggestedPatch.before: Coordinating an Oahu group trip shouldn't be chaos. Here's a day-by-day, friend-proof 7-day itinerary using real spots, plus what actually matters for planning.
suggestedPatch.after: There's a specific moment on every Oahu group trip when someone suggests Hanauma Bay and you realize nobody booked it—and now it's closed Monday, it's Tuesday, and you've lost a day. This itinerary exists to kill that moment before it kills your trip.
